commit.message:
Add Kosaraju's Algorithm blog post for Strongly Connected Components

- Replace SCC placeholder with comprehensive 28-min read blog post
- 15+ SVG diagrams with color-coded SCC regions
- Step-by-step visual walkthrough (20 step cards across 3 phases)
- Interactive animation with Play/Step/Reset controls
- Full C++ implementation with adjacency list
- Complexity analysis and Kosaraju vs Tarjan comparison
- Correctness proof with key lemma on finish times
- Condensation graph (DAG of SCCs) explanation
- Applications: 2-SAT, social networks, compilers, web crawling
- 4 interview problem cards
- Update blog.html with new card entry
- Update blog.js navigation tree

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Prove subgroup_of_cyclic theorem

Add auxiliary lemmas and complete proof of subgroup_of_cyclic:
- gpow_nat_in_subgroup / gpow_in_subgroup: closure of subgroups under powers
- group_order_pos: group order is positive
- well_ordering_nat: well-ordering principle for nat
- generator_period_divides_nat: g^k=e implies m|k
- min_period_in_subgroup: existence of minimal period in subgroup
- subgroup_element_is_power_of_d: elements are powers of the generator
- min_period_divides_group_order: minimal period divides m
- m_div_d_pos: m/d > 0 when d|m
- to_nat_of_nat_lt: Fin conversion lemma
- powers_of_gd_distinct: distinctness of g^(d*i) and g^(d*j)
- every_subgroup_element_is_small_power: each element has index < m/d
- subgroup_group_order: subgroup has order m/d

Also add Logic.ClassicalEpsilon import for
constructive_indefinite_description.

fix: robust CloudTrail Lake teardown — enumerate all orphaned stores

Previously teardown only deleted the single event data store saved to
state, leaving orphaned stores from failed/retried deploys to accumulate
against the 10-store account-wide limit.

Changes:
- Enumerate ALL aria-banking-audit* event data stores (not just state ARN)
- Remove termination protection before each delete (required, otherwise
  deletion throws EventDataStoreTerminationProtectedException)
- Skip protection update for INACTIVE stores (throws
  InactiveEventDataStoreException — delete directly instead)
- Skip stores already in PENDING_DELETION (idempotent)
- Enumerate ALL aria-audit-channel channels (not just state ARN)
- Print clear warning that PENDING_DELETION takes up to 30 min to clear
  from the account limit before re-deploy can create a new store

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(runtime): harden two-node remote ask/reply shutdown (#541)

* test(runtime): add two-node distributed proof-lane integration tests

Add two focused integration tests that exercise the real TCP transport
path end-to-end in a single process:

  - two_node_remote_send_delivery: proves fire-and-forget message
    delivery from node1 to an actor registered on node2.
  - two_node_remote_ask_reply: proves the full ask/reply round-trip
    over the real transport; node1 sends an ask, node2's actor replies,
    and node1 receives the correct result (21 * 2 = 42).

Production seam changes required to make this work correctly:

connection.rs:
  - HewConnMgr gains a local_node_id: u16 field so the handshake
    advertises the correct node ID even when two nodes share a process
    (previously local_handshake() relied on the process-global
    LOCAL_NODE_ID which is only set for CURRENT_NODE).
  - hew_connmgr_new() gains a local_node_id parameter; all call
    sites updated.
  - New pub(crate) hew_connmgr_send_preencoded() sends a pre-encoded
    wire frame through the connection's noise encryption layer, fixing
    a bug where hew_node_api_ask and send_reply_envelope bypassed
    encryption by using the raw transport send directly.
  - New pub(crate) hew_connmgr_conn_id_for_node() lets
    send_reply_envelope look up the accepting side's connection by
    peer_node_id rather than relying on conn_by_node.
  - InboundRouter type gains a *mut HewConnMgr 7th parameter so
    handle_inbound_ask can route replies through the correct conn_mgr.
  - HewConnMgr.transport made pub(crate).

hew_node.rs:
  - hew_connmgr_new call passes node.node_id.
  - hew_node_api_ask switched from raw transport send to
    hew_connmgr_send_preencoded (fixes encryption bypass bug).
  - send_reply_envelope switched from raw transport send to
    hew_connmgr_send_preencoded (fixes encryption bypass bug).
  - node_inbound_router, handle_inbound_ask, send_reply_envelope
    thread the receiving conn_mgr explicitly (fixes reply routing
    on the accepting side where conn_by_node is not populated).

* test(runtime): dedupe two-node proof helpers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(runtime): restore CURRENT_NODE read-lock barrier in send_reply_envelope

ask-handler threads spawned by node_inbound_router captured a raw
*mut HewConnMgr and called send_reply_envelope on a detached thread.
hew_node_stop could call hew_connmgr_free while such a thread was still
executing, resulting in a use-after-free through the freed conn_mgr.

The old design had an implicit barrier: send_reply_envelope held the
CURRENT_NODE read lock and hew_node_stop took the matching write lock
before freeing node state.  That barrier was absent from the
handshake/encryption PR that introduced the encrypted-preencoded send path.

Fix: re-acquire CURRENT_NODE.read_or_recover() at the top of
send_reply_envelope and bail out immediately if *guard == 0.

Protocol of the barrier:
* If the ask-handler thread acquires the read lock first, hew_node_stop's
  write lock (which clears CURRENT_NODE to 0 and precedes hew_connmgr_free)
  must wait until all read-lock-holders release — conn_mgr is valid for the
  whole function body.
* If hew_node_stop already cleared CURRENT_NODE before this thread acquires
  the read lock, the *guard == 0 check causes an early return before any
  pointer dereference of conn_mgr.

hew_connmgr_free is only called after the write lock is released, at which
point no ask-handler thread can be using conn_mgr (either they finished
before the write lock was acquired, or they will see *guard == 0 and bail).

A clarifying comment has been added to the write-lock block in hew_node_stop
to document this invariant.

Validated: cargo test -p hew-runtime two_node_ — 3/3 pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(test): stabilize stress_mass_spawn without weakening concurrency (#539)

* fix(test): replace sleep_ms barrier with await close(w) in stress_mass_spawn

The test was flaky under CI load: sleep_ms(500) is not a reliable barrier
when 500 worker actors each send done() to a shared collector from their
own scheduler tasks. Messages from distinct actors carry no ordering
guarantee relative to main, so get_count() could execute before all
done() messages were delivered — observed as output 499 instead of 500.

Replace the fixed sleep with await close(w) inside the loop, which is an
established Hew drain pattern (see close_await_actor.hew). await close(w)
waits until the worker's mailbox is fully processed, guaranteeing that
collector.done() has been enqueued before the next iteration. After all
500 workers are drained, every done() message is already in the
collector's mailbox; the subsequent await collector.get_count() is
strictly ordered after them via FIFO.

Validated: 5/5 consecutive local runs pass; 52/52 native e2e_actors and
e2e_concurrency tests unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): restore peak-concurrency shape in stress_mass_spawn via two-phase close

The previous fix (await close(w) inline) was correct but serialized worker
lifecycles, dropping peak live-actor count from 500 to ~1 and weakening the
fan-out/fan-in stress profile.

Switch to a two-phase pattern using Vec<ActorRef<Worker>>:
  Phase 1 — spawn all 500 workers and fire work() to every one before any
             close; all workers are live simultaneously, preserving the
             original peak-concurrency stress shape.
  Phase 2 — await close(w) for each worker in the collected Vec; this
             drains every worker's mailbox, guaranteeing all
             collector.done() messages are enqueued before the final
             await collector.get_count() is issued (FIFO on collector).

Vec<ActorRef<Worker>> is valid in current Hew; probe-tested at 500
elements before applying.

Validated: 5/5 consecutive runs produce 500; 55/55 native e2e_actors +
e2e_concurrency + e2e_stress tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(test): use direct worker drain iteration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Optimize nettrace-to-TraceLog Conversion (#2403)

* Use min-heap for EventCache.SortAndDispatch

Replace the O(N*T) linear-scan merge in SortAndDispatch with an O(N*log(T))
min-heap merge, where N is the number of events and T is the number of
threads. The previous implementation rebuilt a List from LINQ on every call
and linearly scanned all thread queues for the minimum timestamp per event.

The new implementation uses an array-backed binary min-heap keyed by
timestamp. After extracting the minimum, only a single O(log T) sift-down
is needed to restore the heap property. The heap list is reused across
calls to avoid per-call allocations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Track active thread queues in EventCache.SortAndDispatch

Maintain a HashSet of thread queues that have pending events instead of
iterating all threads in the dictionary on every SortAndDispatch call.
Queues are added to the active set when their first event is enqueued
and removed when drained. This eliminates the Dictionary.Values
enumeration which was ~28% of CPU during nettrace-to-TraceLog conversion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Cache ParsedSymbolMetadata to avoid repeated JSON deserialization

Cache the result of ProcessMappingSymbolMetadataParser.TryParse() in
ProcessMappingMetadataTraceData so that repeated accesses to the
ParsedSymbolMetadata property do not re-invoke JSON deserialization.
The property is accessed twice per mapping event (for PE and ELF
metadata checks), and the metadata objects are shared across multiple
mappings via MetadataId. This was ~10% of CPU during conversion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Avoid redundant FileName string allocations in ProcessMapping handler

Read ProcessMappingTraceData.FileName once into a local variable and pass
it directly to UniversalMapping(string, ...) instead of going through the
UniversalMapping(ProcessMappingTraceData, ...) overload. Previously,
FileName was accessed 3 times per mapping event (IsNullOrEmpty check,
StartsWith check, and inside UniversalMapping), each time allocating a
new string via GetShortUTF8StringAt(). String allocation was ~12% of CPU
in Release-mode profiling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reset ParsedSymbolMetadata cache in Dispatch to prevent stale data

TraceEvent objects are reused across callbacks. The cached
ParsedSymbolMetadata fields were never cleared between dispatches,
which could return metadata from a previous event if the property
was accessed on the template object rather than a clone.

Reset _parsedSymbolMetadataCached and _parsedSymbolMetadata at the
start of Dispatch() before invoking the callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback: MinHeap class, Clone override, comments

Refactor the min-heap helpers into a self-contained private MinHeap
class with XML doc comments on all public methods. Add comments
explaining the binary heap child index formulas (2i+1, 2i+2). Use
C# tuple swap syntax instead of a temp variable.

Add Clone() override to ProcessMappingMetadataTraceData to explicitly
copy the cached ParsedSymbolMetadata fields into the clone. Strings
are immutable so a shallow copy of the reference is sufficient.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add MinHeap unit tests and improve Build() comment

Make MinHeap generic (MinHeap<TValue>) and internal so it can be
tested from the test project. Add 13 unit tests covering: empty heap,
single element, ascending/descending/random input, duplicate keys,
ReplaceRoot, RemoveRoot, Clear, and mixed operations. Add a comment
to Build() explaining why iteration starts at Count/2-1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add drill phase to lesson runner

Introduce a three-phase lesson architecture in LessonRunner:

Phase 1 (Intro) → Phase 2 (Drill) → Phase 3 (Practice)

The drill phase sits between intro cards and practice exercises,
giving learners reinforcement at the word level before sentence-level
tasks. Words from Phase 1 are queued for multiple-choice drill;
a word is mastered after 3 consecutive correct answers and leaves
the queue. Incorrect answers reset the consecutive count and cycle
the word to the back.

Key additions:
- DrillExercise component (multiple-choice, 4 options, skip support)
- Queue-based mastery algorithm (3 consecutive correct = mastered)
- Segmented progress bar reflecting all three phases independently
- Phase navigation tabs (Intro / Drill / Practice)
- Smart resume logic — reloading lands in the correct phase
- useMemo to split exercises by phase type

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] perf: pre-compute navigation menu structure once per build (O(n²) → O(n)) (#1129)

* perf: pre-compute navigation structure once per build (O(n²) → O(n))

Replace GetNavigationEntries (called once per page) with
GetNavigationEntriesFactory, which pre-computes the expensive
filter/group/sort structure once and returns a cheap closure that
only applies IsActive flags and generates HTML.

For a site with n pages, this reduces:
- sorting/grouping: from O(n × n log n) to O(n log n)
- File.Exists calls for template detection: from 2n to 2
